title: [lichen_l2.c:249-250] atomic_set/get comment falsely claims memory ordering
description: Critic: Comment states 'atomic_set() provides release semantics' and 'atomic_get() provides acquire semantics' but Zephyr's atomic_t operations do NOT guarantee memory ordering. On weakly-ordered architectures (ARM), the compiler or CPU could reorder stores to link_ctx AFTER atomic_set(&link_ctx_initialized, 1), causing races. Either add explicit barriers or use atomic_set_explicit() with memory_order_release.
close_reason: Fixed: corrected misleading comments - Zephyr atomic_set/get do NOT provide memory ordering; explained single-core Cortex-M relies on program order instead
